stories.title: Norris Ends Mercedes' 2026 Qualifying Streak — Sprint Pole in Miami as McLaren Upgrades Bite

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
NORRIS ENDS MERCEDES' QUALIFYING RUN.

McLaren's first pole of 2026. Miami Sprint QF:

1. Norris — 1:27.869
2. Antonelli — +0.222s
3. Piastri — +0.239s

Upgraded McLaren. New floor. New aero. It's working.

#F1Fanrecap #MiamiGP

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
McLaren brought an ENTIRELY NEW CAR to Miami.

New floor. New bodywork. New rear wing. New brake ducts.

Stella called it "completely new from an aerodynamic standpoint."

The result? Their first pole of 2026. #F1Fanrecap #MiamiGP #McLaren

article.headline: Norris Ends Mercedes' 2026 Qualifying Streak — McLaren's Sprint Pole Signals Real Title Threat

stories.title: Miami Sprint Weekend Today — Sprint at Noon, Qualifying Tonight, Race Sunday Under Storm Clouds

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text:
MIAMI F1 SPRINT WEEKEND IS HERE.

Sprint Race: Noon ET today
Qualifying: 5 PM ET tonight
Grand Prix: Sunday 4 PM ET

Norris on sprint pole. Antonelli chasing. McLaren vs Mercedes vs storm clouds.

Let's go. #F1Fanrecap #MiamiGP

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text:
Sunday's Miami GP forecast: 88% rain. 53% thunderstorm chance.

Under US law, the race MUST be halted if lightning gets within 8 miles.

FIA has contingency plans. This could be the most dramatic Miami race ever.

#F1Fanrecap #MiamiGP

article.headline: Miami Sprint Weekend Preview — Sprint at Noon, Qualifying Tonight, Race Sunday Under Storm Clouds

stories.title: McLaren Fires Back With Revolutionary Miami Upgrade — Is This Their 2026 Title Push?

x_posts.label: Post 3-1
x_posts.text:
McLaren won Miami in 2024. They won it in 2025.

Now they're back with the biggest upgrade of 2026 — new floor, bodywork, rear wing, brake ducts — and Norris just took sprint pole.

Three in a row? #F1Fanrecap #McLaren #MiamiGP

x_posts.label: Post 3-2
x_posts.text:
If McLaren's Miami upgrade works on Sunday as well as it did in sprint qualifying, Antonelli's championship lead is in danger.

Gap: Antonelli 72 pts. McLaren (Norris + Piastri) have the pace.

Title fight just got real. #F1Fanrecap #MiamiGP

article.headline: McLaren's Miami Revolution — The Upgrade Package That Could Change the 2026 Championship

stories.title: Antonelli Leads Russell 72-63 — Can Mercedes' Miami Weekend Survive the McLaren Storm?

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
Antonelli: 72 pts. Russell: 63 pts.

But in Miami sprint qualifying, Antonelli was 2nd. Russell was 6th — 0.6 seconds off Norris.

Mercedes still leads but the gap to McLaren is closing fast.

Championship lives on Sunday. #F1Fanrecap #MiamiGP #Mercedes

article.headline: Antonelli 72, Russell 63 — The Miami Sprint Revealed Cracks in Mercedes' Perfect Season

stories.title: Sunday Storm Watch — Will Rain Define the Miami Grand Prix Outcome?

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
Rain. Thunderstorms. US law that can STOP the race.

Sunday's Miami GP is already shaping up as the most chaotic of the year.

88% rain chance. FIA contingency plan. 4 PM ET start.

If it gets wild, ANYTHING can happen. #F1Fanrecap #MiamiGP

article.headline: Miami Storm Watch — 88% Rain, Thunderstorms, and a US Law That Could Stop the Race
